name: V.34-style single-carrier QAM + fractionally-spaced (T/2) LMS FFE + symbol DFE + decision-directed carrier loop

clean_channel_selfcheck.note: RED/GREEN gate: modulate->demodulate with NO channel must invert to BER<1e-3
clean_channel_selfcheck.verdict: PASS - the modem is a true inverse on the trivial channel (FFE+DFE+carrier loop converge, QAM map round-trips)

channel_ablation_diagnosis_tape10_QPSK48.note: data-aided training-MSE (last 300 syms, unit-power signal) with one replay impairment at a time; isolates the DFE killer
channel_ablation_diagnosis_tape10_QPSK48.conclusion: The DOMINANT killer is the measured HF magnitude rolloff (35 dB across 6 kHz). A wide single carrier (fc3500 +/- 3000 Hz = 500-6500 Hz) spans a 35 dB tilt; the upper sideband sits at -35 dB == below the ~35 dB SNR floor, so the linear FFE has nothing to equalize there (inverting a 35 dB notch blows up noise). The convolutional impairments the DFE was supposed to handle (diffuse reverb, adjacent-bin ISI, flutter, phase jitter) are EACH individually well-equalized (MSE<=0.02) -- the DFE works; the band placement is fatal.
